text.title: Shift Log Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Shift Log Template methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every shift with at least one state change MUST close with a shift-log artefact conforming to the schema. No log = shift not closed.
rule.rationale: Without a closing artefact, handover state is verbal and lossy.

rule.statement: The log MUST name the next operator (single named person or rotating named role) AND a reachable channel (handle + DM URL).
rule.rationale: 'Team' / 'we' is not addressable; the next responder must be identifiable.

rule.statement: The log MUST contain a `pickup_first` field (≤200 chars) that tells the next operator exactly what to touch first, including artefact paths.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit pickup pointer, the next operator burns the first 30 minutes orienting.

rule.statement: All open items (incidents, decisions, blocked dependencies) MUST be listed with owner + due date. Empty list is allowed; omission is not.
rule.rationale: Open items left implicit get dropped between shifts.

rule.statement: The log MUST be scrubbed for secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before publish. Validator runs a regex sweep on stringy fields.
rule.rationale: Shift logs are typically posted in semi-public channels.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-shift-log-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
